response:
The inequality for the first requirement is 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 >= 10. Evaluating the function P(x) at the boundaries of the interval [3, 6] shows that P(3) equals 2(27) - 5(9) + 3(3) - 7 which is 54 - 45 + 9 - 7 equaling 11. Since the derivative P'(x) = 6x^2 - 10x + 3 is positive for all x >= 3, the function is strictly increasing in this interval. Because the minimum value at x=3 is 11, which is greater than 10, the condition is satisfied for the entire range from 3 to 6 inches.

******
To find the screen width where the area is 15 square inches, we solve 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 = 15, which simplifies to 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 22 = 0. Testing integer values reveals that x=3 yields 54 - 45 + 9 - 22 = -4, while x=4 yields 128 - 80 + 12 - 22 = 38. Using numerical methods or the cubic formula to approximate the real root between 3 and 4 yields a value of approximately 3.28 inches. The other two roots are complex and do not represent physical screen widths.
